 {"id":"bd-3dca","title":"Session Recording to Asciicast Format","description":"# Session Recording to Asciicast Format

## Background & Motivation

Recording terminal sessions enables:
- Bug reports with full reproduction
- Demo videos for documentation
- Shareable examples via asciinema.org

**The solution:**
- Record all terminal output in asciicast v2 format
- Compatible with asciinema-player for web embedding
- Integrate with Time-Travel Debugging for offline replay

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Extends bd-2lwf (Time-Travel Debugging) with export capability
- Enhances bd-2fx (export adapters) with asciicast output

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct AsciicastRecorder {
    output: BufWriter<File>,
    start_time: Instant,
    terminal_size: (u16, u16),
}

impl AsciicastRecorder {
    pub fn new(path: &Path, width: u16, height: u16) -> io::Result<Self> {
        let header = json!({
            "version": 2,
            "width": width,
            "height": height,
            "timestamp": Utc::now().timestamp()
        });
        // Write header...
    }
    
    pub fn record_output(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
        let time = self.start_time.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
        let event = json!([time, "o", String::from_utf8_lossy(data)]);
        writeln!(self.output, "{}", event).ok();
    }
    
    pub fn finish(self) -> io::Result<()> {
        self.output.flush()
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Recording start (INFO level)
tracing::info!(path = ?path, width = width, height = height, "Asciicast recording started");

// Output recorded (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(bytes = data.len(), elapsed_secs = time, "Output recorded");

// Recording complete (INFO level)
tracing::info!(path = ?path, duration_secs = duration, events = event_count, "Asciicast recording complete");
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Record a session
./ftui-harness --record-asciicast session.cast --duration 10s

# Validate asciicast format
asciinema cat session.cast > /dev/null && echo "Valid format"

# Verify playback
asciinema play session.cast --speed 2

# Test with asciinema-player web component
./scripts/test-asciicast-player.sh session.cast
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Asciicast v2 format output (NDJSON)
- [ ] Plays correctly in asciinema-player
- [ ] Timing preserved accurately (sub-ms precision)
- [ ] Works with large recordings (1hr+)
- [ ] Integration with ftui runtime
- [ ] Input events optionally recorded
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:11:11.935650561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:31:59.489446043Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:31:59.489378395Z","close_reason":"AsciicastRecorder implemented with v2 format, NDJSON output, input/output recording, JSON escaping, and AsciicastLoader for playback (20 tests passing)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dca","depends_on_id":"bd-2fx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3dca","depends_on_id":"bd-2lwf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Added suite hook in tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh. Inline UI height raised to 18 for visibility. Script passes locally.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralBarn","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:17:46.274255445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:31:59.299943756Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T01:31:59.299923608Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","e2e","logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dh8m","depends_on_id":"bd-22n80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:17:48.903876194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3dh8m","depends_on_id":"bd-2txo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:17:48.664800467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3dh8m","depends_on_id":"bd-3isi7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:17:49.142412606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t","title":"Alien Artifact Efficiency & Power Upgrade","description":"Background
- FrankenTUI already includes principled control in render budgeting (PID + e-process) and VOI sampling. This epic layers additional mathematically rigorous mechanisms to improve performance and power, especially on large, high-resolution terminals, while preserving determinism and correctness.

Goals
- Reduce diff/present overhead on large screens without sacrificing correctness.
- Improve resize handling under bursty input with formal regime detection.
- Add distribution-free frame-time risk control to prevent budget overruns.
- Keep behavior deterministic and explainable with evidence ledgers.

Non-Goals
- No ML black boxes. No non-deterministic heuristics. No compatibility shims.

Success Criteria
- Rendering remains flicker-free and deterministic in inline and alt-screen modes.
- Performance scales gracefully with large terminal sizes (e.g., 200x60+).
- Unit + property tests validate invariants, and e2e harness scenarios pass.
- All new decisions have explainable, logged evidence (JSONL).

Notes
- API changes are allowed. Prefer explicit configuration toggles with safe defaults.
- Maintain one-writer rule and dirty-row invariants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:15:57.781614542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:48:49.237474018Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:48:49.237431468Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.19.1","title":"frankenterm-web: shaping feature-gate config scaffold (default off)","description":"Introduce explicit wasm-facing text-shaping configuration scaffold with default disabled behavior. Add parser/state snapshot plumbing and tests proving default-off + deterministic toggling so future Harfbuzz shaping can land behind a hard feature gate with no default perf impact.","notes":"Added additional shaping-gate test coverage in crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs:
- set_text_shaping_rejects_non_boolean_values
- destroy_restores_text_shaping_default_state
- text_shaping_toggle_keeps_patch_projection_deterministic

These strengthen bd-lff4p.2.19.1 by proving invalid input rejection, destroy reset semantics, and no behavioral drift in patch/autolink projections while shaping toggle is plumbing-only.

- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-run","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:02:48.493065913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:22:10.833480779Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:16:54.571463208Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.19.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:02:48.493065913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.19.2","title":"frankenterm-web: harden text-shaping gate invariants + no-op determinism tests","description":"Goal
- Make the shaping feature-gate scaffold safer and more explicit before engine implementation.

Scope
- Add wasm tests proving: (1) non-boolean shaping config values are rejected, (2) reset restores default shaping state, and (3) toggling shaping does not alter current patch projection/autolink behavior (deterministic no-op).
- Keep runtime behavior unchanged (no shaping engine yet).

Validation
- cargo test -p frankenterm-web text_shaping -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-run
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo fmt --check","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryHawk","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:17:52.297144577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:21:24.147262460Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:21:24.147241732Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.19.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:17:52.297144577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","title":"Implement: new crate frankenterm-web (wasm-bindgen API + build plumbing)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/frankenterm-web` with WASM bindings and a minimal JS-facing API.

Requirements
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Expose: init(canvas_id or handle), resize, feed_input, present_patch.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","title":"Optional: auto URL detection (non-OSC8) + safe click handling","description":"Goal
- Detect plain-text URLs in the viewport/scrollback and make them clickable (similar to xterm.js WebLinks addon).

Scope
- Heuristics tuned to avoid false positives in code.
- Security: sanitize and enforce allowed protocols.

Testing
- Fixture corpus for common URL forms.
- E2E: click opens expected target (or emits link event) with JSONL logs.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.21","title":"frankenterm-web scroll: avoid no-op inertia animation + deterministic stop test","description":"Background: scroll.rs currently seeds inertia animation even when wheel input produces zero whole-line movement (fractional-only updates), causing avoidable extra animation frames. The inertia stop test also uses panic-on-loop style.\n\nScope:\n- In crates/frankenterm-web/src/scroll.rs, only enable inertia animation when there is meaningful motion to animate.\n- Add/adjust unit tests so no-op wheel deltas do not start animation and inertia-stop behavior is validated with deterministic bounded assertions (no panic loop).\n- Keep behavior deterministic and avoid altering external API semantics.","notes":"Implemented in crates/frankenterm-web/src/scroll.rs:\n- apply_wheel now seeds inertia only when wheel input causes actual offset movement and non-zero line_delta; clamped/no-op wheel inputs clear animating/velocity instead of starting inertial frames.\n- Replaced panic-loop in inertia_decays_to_stop with deterministic bounded assertion (<=500 ticks).\n- Added tests: wheel_fractional_noop_does_not_start_inertia, wheel_clamped_movement_does_not_start_inertia.\n\nValidation:\n- cargo test -p frankenterm-web scroll -- --nocapture\n- cargo check --all-targets (workspace)\n- cargo fmt --check -p frankenterm-web\n- cargo clippy -p frankenterm-web --all-targets -- -D warnings\n\nWorkspace-wide fmt/clippy currently fail on unrelated concurrent edits in other files (e.g., crates/frankenterm-core/tests/scroll_region_fixtures.rs, crates/frankenterm-web/src/patch_feed.rs).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-09T13:10:21.126337359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:14:51.064338555Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:14:51.064316674Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.21","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T13:10:21.126337359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
